title: Stakeholders, Ethics, Accessibility, and Harm Analysis · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs stakeholders, ethics, accessibility, and harm analysis from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.
Apply public good, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, competence, review, and accountability to concrete project conflicts.
Treat disability, language, literacy, device, connectivity, environment, and support needs as core system requirements and research conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Stakeholder Power Map · changed case · Review Stakeholder Power Map
steps.instruction:
review case — Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

Which action demonstrates Stakeholder Power Map without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Stakeholder Power Map · changed case · Review Stakeholder Power Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users. For Stakeholder Power Map · changed case · Review Stakeholder Power Map, keep Stakeholder Power Map connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Stakeholder Power Map condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing

At Stakeholder Power Map · changed case · Review Stakeholder Power Map, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

For Stakeholder Power Map · changed case · Review Stakeholder Power Map, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Stakeholder importance is proportional to purchasing power.

options.text: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.
Stakeholder importance is proportional to purchasing power. That claim does not survive the stakeholder power map · changed case evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

stimulus.title: Ethics Review Route case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A software team can resolve every domain risk through technical controls.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims. Artifact under review: stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Ethics Review Route, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Ethics Review Route
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Ethics Review Route: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.

For Record what required effort · Review Ethics Review Route, keep Ethics Review Route connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.
